questions.question_text: Which of the following substances is a compound?
questions.explanation: A compound consists of two or more different elements chemically combined in a fixed ratio. Oxygen, magnesium and sodium are all elements but water is a compound made up of hydrogen and oxygen (H 2 O)

questions.question_text: An atom of an element has a neutral charge because the

options.A: Protons and electrons are the same particles.
options.B: Proton number and electron number are the same.
options.C: Neutron number and proton number are equal.
options.D: Electron number and neutron number are equal.

questions.explanation: Neutral Atom : have a charge balance, which means that they have an equal number of protons and electrons.

questions.question_text: Which of the following organisms is an ecto-parasite of animals?
questions.explanation:
Ectoparasite : parasites that live on the surfaces of host organisms or in the skin and do not invade the inner body

 Examples of ectoparasites 

Fleas, head lice and bed bugs

 Endoparasite 

 Endoparasites are parasites which live inside a host and generally inhabit areas such as the gut, lungs,

questions.question_text: An atom has 20 nucleons and 9 protons. What is its neutron number?
questions.explanation:
Nucleon = Proton + Neutron

Neutron = Nucleon - Proton

Neutron = 20 - 9 = 11

questions.question_text: An aluminium cube of side 2 m has a mass 24 kg. Determine the density of the aluminium.
questions.explanation:
Density = Mass / Volume

Volume = Length x Breadth x Height

Breadth = Height = Length for cube

Volume of a cube = Length x Length x Length = Length 3 

Volume of the aluminium = 2 3 = 2 x 2 x 2 = 8 m 3 

Density = 24 kg/ 8 m 3 = 3 kg m -3

questions.question_text: Which of the following chemical symbols is that of an element with seven electrons in the outermost shell?
questions.explanation: Electronic configuration for Chlorine ( 17 Cl) is 2,8, 7 . As you can see the last shell (outermost shell) has 7 electrons

questions.question_text: Which of the following fruits is dispersed by water?
questions.explanation:
Coconut is dispersed by water

Lighter and hollow seeds like that of coconut and lotus can float in water and travel long distances in this manner.

questions.question_text: The function of the white blood cells in humans is to

options.B: ensure blood clot during an injury.
options.C: produce antibodies to engulf disease-causing organisms.

questions.explanation: White blood cells produce antibodies to fight the infection and help with other immune responses.

questions.explanation:
Organic Farming

Organic farming can be defined as an agricultural process that uses biological fertilizers and pest control acquired from animal or plant waste.

questions.question_text: Which of the following planets is at the center of the solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the only star in our solar system. It is the center of our solar system, and its gravity holds the solar system together.

questions.question_text: Determine the potential energy of a block of mass 2 kg placed on a building 10 m tall. [g = 10 ms -2 ]
questions.explanation:
Potential energy = mass x acceleration due to gravity x height

P.E = mgh

P.E = 2 x 10 x 10 J = 200 J

questions.question_text: Which of the following organisms attacks crops in storage?
questions.explanation:
A storage pest is an insect or other animal that damages or destroys stored food or other stored valuable organic matter.

Grain borers, weevils and Angoumois grain moths are able to feed on whole, healthy grains, they are considered storage pests

options.B: have no effect on blue litmus paper.
options.C: turn wet red litmus paper blue.

questions.explanation:
Testing for acid

 Blue litmus paper will turn red in acid solutions and red litmus blue in base solutions.

questions.question_text: Which of the following elements is a macro-nutrient of plants?
questions.explanation:
Macro-nutrients are required by plants in large amounts and micronutrients are required in trace amounts .

questions.question_text: When an atom loses an electron, the ion formed is called
questions.explanation:
Ion : is a charged atom or a group of atoms which possesses an electric charge.

 Cation : The ion formed when an atom loses electron(s) is known as cation e.g. Na + , Mg 2+

 Anion : The ion formed when an atom gains electron(s) is known as anion e.g Cl - , O 2-

questions.question_text: Which of the following chemical equations is balanced?
questions.explanation:
Number of Na at left = 2 (2 x 1) and at the right = 2 (2 x 1)

Number of Cl at left = 2 and at the right = 2 (2 x 1)

Since the number of elements at each side of the equation is the same, the equation is balanced

questions.explanation:
Cholera : is an acute diarrheal illness caused by infection of the intestine with Vibrio cholerae bacteria.

Deficiency Diseases

Deficiency diseases are diseases that are caused by the lack of certain essential nutrients in one's diet over a prolonged period of time.

questions.question_text: In an experiment to determine the various particle sizes of soil sedimentation, the particle above clay suspension are

Heat from the sun by

I. Conduction

II. Convention

III. Radiation
questions.explanation:
Radiation : is the transfer of heat energy through space by electromagnetic radiation.

 Conduction : is the transfer of heat energy from one substance to another or within a substance

 Convection : is the transfer of heat energy in a fluid.
